source_evidence: Appendix B.3, Eq. (20)-(23): quadrangle split into two triangles; n = AB1 x AB2; t_w = (PA.n)/(d.n); triple products V_a=[d PC PB1]=e1.PB1, V_b=[d PC PB2]=e1.PB2, V2=-[d PC PA]=-e1.PA with e1=d x PC; choose correct triangle by sign(V_a)/sign(V_b) vs sign(V2), then V3=e2.PA; collides iff 0<=t_w<=1 and sign(V2)=sign(V3).
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects a flat quadrangle restriction primitive by splitting it into two triangles and reusing the triangle test, with an early-out to save computation.
assumptions: Quadrangle is flat and all 4 points lie (approximately) in the same plane
Quadrangle is an extension of the triangle primitive case
One of the two triangles can be chosen early to save computation
formula_or_algorithm: d = Q - P. Plane normal n = AB1 x AB2. t_w = (PA . n)/(d . n). With e1 = d x PC compute V_a = e1.PB1, V_b = e1.PB2, V2 = -e1.PA. Pick the correct sub-triangle: e2 = d x PB1 if sign(V_a)=sign(V2), else e2 = d x PB2 if sign(V_b)=sign(V2); then V3 = e2 . PA. The segment collides with quadrangle AB1CB2 iff 0 <= t_w <= 1 AND sign(V2) = sign(V3).
validation_opportunities: unit tests over both sub-triangles and the shared diagonal
consistency with two independent full triangle tests

reuse_risk.explanation: Depends on the flat/coplanar assumption for its 4 vertices; the early-triangle-selection sign logic is easy to implement incorrectly.

missing_information: Behavior when the 4 points are not exactly coplanar
Sign-tie handling on the shared diagonal
